note: Candidate D1 is proposed as new production candidate per pre-registered criteria, but final adoption (including the size tradeoff) is left to the coordinator/user.
shaTranscriptionNote: The task file's candidate SHA-256 (...bbfffcf4caf20fc9) differs by one segment from the manifest's value (...bbfff4cf4caf20fc9); per instructions the manifest value was used as authoritative, and it matched the on-disk file exactly.

baselineReuseSpotCheck.method: ran v5 weights with --opening-limit 3 (6 games) at current HEAD (eval_cli rebuilt), compared against the first 6 games of T166's candidate C run (bench/edax-compare/endgame-results/t166-c-vs-edax-results-full.json)
baselineReuseSpotCheck.marginAndPliesMatch: all 6 games matched exactly (primary-01/black -32, primary-01/white +8, primary-02/black -6, primary-02/white -10, primary-03/black -40, primary-03/white +32; plies also matched)

moveNodesDiscDiffMismatch.description: from ply 33 (empties=17) the position was already fully solved (discDiff pinned at +32.0 in both runs), but starting at ply 34 the exact sequence of moves chosen among the remaining forced/tied squares diverged between the two eval_cli binaries, along with differing node counts per move (discDiff itself stayed identical at every step)
moveNodesDiscDiffMismatch.likelyCause: T168 added new pattern shapes (corner5x2/diag4) to the engine's internal pattern table, which plausibly shifted move-enumeration/tie-break order among equally-valued moves in an already-determined endgame position; this does not affect search correctness or final outcome
moveNodesDiscDiffMismatch.impactOnFinalResult: none -- margin and plies for this game matched exactly (+32, 49 plies)

baselineReuseSpotCheck.decision: Per the task's explicit rule (even one mismatch -> run fresh), T166's candidate C 60-game data was NOT reused; v5 baseline was executed fresh for the full 60 games.

candidateD1ScalarFeaturePrecheck.method: standalone eval_cli invocation before the 60-game run: `eval_cli gen` for the standard initial position, then `eval_cli best --pattern-weights <candidate D1 path> ...` with the same protocol parameters, capturing stderr
candidateD1ScalarFeaturePrecheck.v5Baseline: scalar_features_present=true scalar_features_enabled=true (v5 is T166 candidate C, which already carries scalar features from the B3 lineage)
candidateD1ScalarFeaturePrecheck.note: vs_edax.py's run() helper captures subprocess stderr internally and only surfaces it on failure, so this diagnostic does not appear in the 60-game run's own logs; the standalone pre-check satisfies this requirement.

D1.allowDirtyReason: worktree dirty only from an in-progress tasks/T169-edax-pattern-game-gate.md work-log edit (documentation only)

statistics.bootstrapArrayOrderingNote: Opening-level arrays ordered by opening number ascending (primary-01..primary-30). Game-level arrays ordered by (opening number ascending, then black before white). Identical convention to T166/T162.
statistics.algorithmNote: Identical to bench/edax-compare/compare_pattern_v3.py#paired_bootstrap and to T158d/T162/T166 reports.
statistics.diffDefinition: D1 opening-average (or game) margin minus v5 opening-average (or game) margin

adoptionCriterionApplication.criterionText: CI entirely above 0 (significant improvement) AND 0 anomalies -> propose adoption
adoptionCriterionApplication.proposal: Candidate D1 (V3+corner5x2) is proposed as the new production candidate.

sizeTradeoffUserDecision.note: Per task requirement, this size increase (~1.83x gzip) is explicitly flagged as a user decision point: whether the statistically significant strength gain (+4.53 discs/game point estimate) justifies the larger download/load size for the app. This worker does not render a verdict on this tradeoff.

wallInsuranceAndTiming.wallInsuranceDefinition: wallInsuranceFired = (engine move's timedOut == true) AND (nodeLimitHit == false), identical to vs_edax.py's policy_calibration wallInsuranceFired definition
wallInsuranceAndTiming.conclusion: 0 wall-insurance triggers on both arms across all engine moves; the 160000-node budget consistently binds before the 1500ms wall-clock budget even with D1's ~21% lower NPS (per T168's measurement), so the NPS reduction did not translate into any time-based search truncation in this gate.

timePerGameComparison.note: D1 is about 12% slower per game on average, less than the ~21% NPS reduction reported in T168, likely because the node budget (not raw NPS) is the binding constraint for most of the budgeted phase.

exactFallbackDefinition.note: Counts ONLY the single transition move (the last budgeted-phase move before the engine's root empties drops to <= unlimited_exact_empties=20). Identical definition to T158d/T162/T166.

budgetedToExactDeviation.interpretation: D1 shows a slightly smaller and more stable deviation than v5 (mean 4.31 vs 4.79); no sign of search-quality degradation from the NPS reduction.

signFlips.conclusion: All flips originate from small near-zero budgeted values (largest magnitude 9.90, D1 primary-22/white); no catastrophic reversal on either side. D1 has fewer flips than v5, consistent with its slightly more stable deviations above.

proposal: Candidate D1 (V3+corner5x2, PWV6) is proposed as the new production candidate per pre-registered criteria (opening-level 95% CI entirely above 0: [1.7833, 7.3333]; 0 anomalies). The gzip size increase (~5.9MB -> ~10.7MB, +82%) is an explicit user decision point not resolved by this worker.
outOfScope: final adoption decision (including the size tradeoff)
